commands.author: Pick-cat

target.title: fix(ui): preserve live tool stream order
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes live WebChat ordering when a tool event timestamp is older than the assistant stream segment that introduced that tool call.
- Threads optional `toolCallId` metadata through chat stream segment types so the renderer can keep the matching tool card after its causal assistant text while preserving ordinary timestamp ordering.
- Adds regression coverage for both the delayed/live timestamp race and same-millisecond multi-segment ordering behind #92122.

Fixes #92122

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.ui.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-ui.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: WebChat live streaming can render a tool card before the assistant text that triggered it when the tool message timestamp sorts earlier than the stream segment.

Real environment tested: Local Control UI rendering/model unit tests in the OpenClaw checkout on Node 22 via repo test wrappers.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts` with regression cases where `streamSegments[n].toolCallId` matches a tool card whose timestamp is older than the segment, including same-millisecond multi-segment ordering.

Evidence after fix: The regressions assert rendered chat items remain `[assistant stream segment, matching tool card]`, and `[first stream segment, first tool card, second stream segment, second tool card]` when two matching stream segments share the same timestamp. I also ran the production Control UI chat-item projection directly after the patch:

```json
{
  "behavior": "live Control UI chat item projection",
  "scenario": "tool timestamp is older than the assistant stream segment that introduced it",
  "visibleOrder": [
    "stream:I will inspect the file.",
    "tool:call-read"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The live projection output shows the assistant stream segment before the matching tool card, and focused chat item tests passed: 35 tests in `ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts`.

What was not tested: I did not run a full live Gateway session or produce a video artifact. The broader DOM chat render and controller tests passed locally, and `chat-responsive.browser.test.ts` is skipped in this local environment.
